mnemonics.title: Myth Categories Rememberer
mnemonics.content: MAP: Management, Adversary (Customer), Practitioner - the three pillars of software myths

acronyms.expansion: Adding N programmers to a late project makes it (N+1) times later
acronyms.usage: Use this to explain why adding resources to failing projects doesn't work

acronyms.expansion: Scope, Time, Cost - the three interdependent project constraints
acronyms.usage: Use this to explain project tradeoffs and why all three cannot be optimized simultaneously

visualTips.visualization: Draw a curved line showing diminishing returns as more programmers are added, with the curve going up (more delays) after a point
Imagine a triangle with 'Scope' on top, 'Time' on bottom-left, 'Cost' on bottom-right - show that pushing any side inward pushes others outward
Three overlapping circles showing Management, Customer, and Practitioner myths with example myths inside each

analogies.analogy: Like a house renovation - while walls can be moved, each change requires checking plumbing, electrical, and structural integrity
Like adding more cooks to a delayed meal - more people in the kitchen actually slows things down due to crowding and coordination
Like planning a vacation - even with detailed plans, you discover new places and change preferences as you research
